title: Memory Leak Learning Resources
author: androiddevnotes
body:
(Draft)

## Setup

[Getting Started - LeakCanary](https://square.github.io/leakcanary/getting_started/)

## Tutorial

### Practical demonstration of identifying Memory Leaks in Mozilla Firefox (Fenix on GitHub)

Shows how to read the "leak trace", how to identify memory leaks.

[The Sandbox: LeakCanary - YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kHHOhPPRytc&t=1532s)

### General info and what's new in LeakCanary 2

[Ask the Expert #2: LeakCanary 2.x is out, track your memory leaks - YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Sx0k4ipqwBs)

## Tools

[The Android Profiler  |  Android Developers](https://developer.android.com/studio/profile/android-profiler) - Only Memory section of Profiler is relevant.

[LeakCanary](https://square.github.io/leakcanary/)

## Code

### Reproducible Sample of Memory Leak

Run the app in Android Studio.

[maxkohne/Android-Memory-Leaks: This is an example of how memory leaks happen with fragments in Android. It also has a solution to the problem.](https://github.com/maxkohne/Android-Memory-Leaks)

## Notes

Frequent, consistent Garbage Collection in Android Studio Profiler (Memory) is not good.

CoordinatorLayout leaked does not necessarily mean that you should look into the XML code of CoordinatorLayout. Refer to the [The Sandbox: LeakCanary - YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kHHOhPPRytc) to see how to read such leak trace.

At times, when using the Android Studio profiler (memory), you may want to disable or comment out LeakCanary dependency from your build gradle file. (Not necessarily).

## Different Types of Memory Leak and Reference

The list is not exhaustive.

Note, not all code is tested. But helpful references.

### RecyclerView adapter null

[fenix/AddonsManagementFragment.kt at 7070bb5607f5ac9fd0825ab21e7abba675f98bdc · mozilla-mobile/fenix](https://github.com/mozilla-mobile/fenix/blob/7070bb5607f5ac9fd0825ab21e7abba675f98bdc/app/src/main/java/org/mozilla/fenix/addons/AddonsManagementFragment.kt#L90-L93)

[How to Fix Memory Leak in RecyclerView (LeakCanary) - YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nNL22A4dIqg&t=11s)

### AlertDialog Memory Leak

[florisboard/TypingFragment.kt at 9a146ba2f0040e8cf1652cd21dd358268cda3759 · florisboard/florisboard](https://github.com/florisboard/florisboard/blob/9a146ba2f0040e8cf1652cd21dd358268cda3759/app/src/main/java/dev/patrickgold/florisboard/settings/fragments/TypingFragment.kt#L45-L48)

[android - Leak Canary detects memory leaks for TabLayout with ViewPager2 - Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/61779776/leak-canary-detects-memory-leaks-for-tablayout-with-viewpager2)

### Using backing field pattern (when necessary)

[The Sandbox: LeakCanary - YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kHHOhPPRytc&t=1451s)

### Need to close actionMode if contextual actionMode is showing.

[food-recipes/FavoriteRecipesFragment.kt at 3a66c9b796d99cd5926f7743f2699c470d5acbb4 · ZattWine/food-recipes](https://github.com/ZattWine/food-recipes/blob/3a66c9b796d99cd5926f7743f2699c470d5acbb4/app/src/main/java/com/norm/foodrecipes/ui/fragments/favorites/FavoriteRecipesFragment.kt#L72-L73)

### setOnRefreshListener null

[SkateShare/FeedFragment.kt at ab2d4499b841b6c8cb6ea2f3d261570e8f05b336 · avhagedorn/SkateShare](https://github.com/avhagedorn/SkateShare/blob/ab2d4499b841b6c8cb6ea2f3d261570e8f05b336/app/src/main/java/com/skateshare/views/feed/FeedFragment.kt#L166)

### searchView.setOnQueryTextListener(null)

[GreedyNewsApp/NewsFragment.kt at e3b290a07d53d83a5b5a8dd5fb60e9a6fcf8bd04 · ShobhitCoder/GreedyNewsApp](https://github.com/ShobhitCoder/GreedyNewsApp/blob/e3b290a07d53d83a5b5a8dd5fb60e9a6fcf8bd04/app/src/main/java/greedy/newsapidemo/ui/main/news/NewsFragment.kt#L170)

### drawerHelper.removeDrawerListener(listener)

[Tv/MeFragment.kt at 3c22f2baad651c3df53b3615fd6fc28263621a42 · bytebyte6/Tv](https://github.com/bytebyte6/Tv/blob/3c22f2baad651c3df53b3615fd6fc28263621a42/tv/src/main/java/com/bytebyte6/view/me/MeFragment.kt#L146-L147)

### Dismiss Dialog (set to null) and Input Method Manager

[NfcDemo/MainActivity.kt at 2cce6dc82fddc18c1c00aa04cdee25b300ecb77a · ewgcat/NfcDemo](https://github.com/ewgcat/NfcDemo/blob/2cce6dc82fddc18c1c00aa04cdee25b300ecb77a/app/src/main/java/com/lishuaihua/nfcsample/MainActivity.kt#L86-L87)

### viewpager2

ViewPager2 internally uses RecyclerView I think, so use the same technique to prevent memory leaks as you would use for RecyclerView adapter.

[ViewPager2 holds on to FragmentStateAdapter after detach [151212195] - Visible to Public - Issue Tracker](https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/151212195#comment3)

[NavigationComponents-Tutorials/ViewPagerContainerFragment.kt at c2fcaf175fbd99183d424d1281a47275e1101f85 · SmartToolFactory/NavigationComponents-Tutorials](https://github.com/SmartToolFactory/NavigationComponents-Tutorials/blob/c2fcaf175fbd99183d424d1281a47275e1101f85/Tutorial8-2DynamicFeatures-ComplexArchitecture/src/main/java/com/smarttoolfactory/tutorial8_2dynamicfeatures_complexarchitecture/fragment/viewpagerfragment/ViewPagerContainerFragment.kt)

### Do it before super.onDestroyView (!?)

Not sure if before or after nulling the view is preferred. People do either ways. 

[architecture-components-samples/InflateFragment.kt at main · android/architecture-components-samples](https://github.com/android/architecture-components-samples/blob/main/ViewBindingSample/app/src/main/java/com/android/example/viewbindingsample/InflateFragment.kt)

[Avoid references to the Fragment's Views after onDestroyView() by ianhanniballake · Pull Request #785 · android/architecture-components-samples](https://github.com/android/architecture-components-samples/pull/785/files)

title: Remove Android Security empty link
author: zeusalmighty717
body:
- At Line 55 there is an empty link for ```Android security```.
- At this time only 'The Mobile Security Testing Guide (MSTG)' is related to security and doesn't make sense to keep it in a separate section.

title: You should have more basic sorts of projects which are more suitable for beginner-ish learners.
author: 103sbavert
body: All the projects in this repo are awesome and they seem really promising. However I do have one problem, all of these projects are just way too high level. For a person like me, I don't know how MVVM works or RxJava works and these projects use those kinds of things which are way out of league for me. And sure as hell I should learn those things but right now I want to see the usage of more basic stuff. Either the use of MVVM in more basic and simple ways, or apps that don't use the stuff I don't understand. Like for example a basic app that implements the androix and navigation components. THese are the things beginners might need to see the best practices of instead very advanced features like RxJava and Architectural Patterns. I would like to see apps implement the latest Jetpack libraries and the material design library with best practices. These things are not too high level but they are also very important to learn.

title: Pull Requests / Contributions are welcomed!
author: androiddevnotes
body:
If you want to contribute android learning resources to awesome-android-learning-resources, Pull Requests are welcomed!

If you are a beginner looking for your first contribution to Open Source, I will help you with Pull Request and contributing guide. 🐣

Feel free to send Pull Requests, fix typos, grammatical mistakes ...

title: Request to add Android Library/SDK development learning resource
author: droid-ash
